user_query:
Please automate an inspection-and-rewrite pass on my Model UN position paper drafts to help me prepare counterarguments. The inputs are the three text files under input/drafts/. Produce the following outputs under the specified paths. Do not modify files in input/. Create any missing output directories.

Scope and detection rules:
- Track the following policy keywords (case-insensitive, match as whole words/phrases): ["tariff", "subsidy", "free trade", "sanction", "carbon tax", "cap-and-trade", "capital controls", "privatization", "state-owned", "austerity"].
- Plural handling: treat "tariffs" as "tariff", "subsidies" as "subsidy", and "sanctions" as "sanction" when counting.
- Count exact whole-word occurrences of "support" and "oppose" (case-insensitive). A document has a contradiction if both "support" and "oppose" appear at least once anywhere in the document.

1) Structured analysis (required):
- Write output/analysis/keywords.json as a JSON array. Include one object per input document with exactly these fields:
  - doc_id: the input filename without extension (e.g., "position_paper_trade").
  - source_path: relative path to the input file (e.g., "input/drafts/position_paper_trade.txt").
  - total_words: integer count of whitespace-delimited tokens in the document.
  - keyword_counts: an object mapping each of the 10 tracked keywords to an integer count as detected by the rules above.
  - support_count: integer count of the word "support".
  - oppose_count: integer count of the word "oppose".
  - has_contradiction: boolean true if support_count > 0 and oppose_count > 0, otherwise false.

2) Status summary report (required):
- Write output/reports/status_summary.md. For each document, include a short section that states: doc_id, whether has_contradiction is true/false, and the top 2 keywords by count for that document (if ties, break alphabetically). End the report with an overall metrics section that lists: total documents processed and, for each tracked keyword, the number of documents in which it appears at least once.

3) Counterpoint rewrites (required):
- For each input file in input/drafts/, create an edited copy at output/revisions/{doc_id}_counterpoint.md that contains the full original text followed by an appended section titled "Counterpoint".
- In the "Counterpoint" section, include exactly one bullet for each tracked keyword that has a count > 0 in that document. Each bullet must follow this exact template: "- Counterpoint on <keyword> in <doc_id>: prepare arguments challenging reliance on <keyword>."
- Preserve the original content above the new section.

4) Action items notes (required):
- Write output/notes/action_items.md with one subsection per document titled "Action Items — {doc_id}" and exactly three bullets per document:
  1) "Prepare talking points for the top keyword: <keyword>" where <keyword> is the single most frequent tracked keyword in that doc (if ties, choose the alphabetically first among the tied keywords; if no keywords are present, write "none").
  2) If has_contradiction is true: "Clarify stance conflicts detected (support vs oppose) in <doc_id>"; otherwise: "Add anticipatory Q&A covering counter-arguments for <doc_id>".
  3) "Research and draft counterpoints for a missing keyword: <keyword>" where <keyword> is the alphabetically first keyword from the tracked list with count = 0 in that doc (if none are missing, write "none").

Notes:
- Keep all processing local; do not use the internet.
- Use only the provided input files. Ensure that keyword counting and contradiction detection follow the rules above so results can be cross-checked against the inputs.

Input path(s): input/drafts/*.txt
Expected output path(s):
- output/analysis/keywords.json
- output/reports/status_summary.md
- output/revisions/{doc_id}_counterpoint.md for each input file
- output/notes/action_items.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
